tests.title: Correct build under test
tests.requires: You installed the release intended for this test pass.
tests.expected: The control panel version banner shows the build under test (0.10.2), so every result maps to the right code. A stale install must never masquerade as a bug.

steps.text: Open the control panel (http://127.0.0.1:8765) or the packaged app window.
Confirm the version banner reads 0.10.2 - it must match the installer you just ran.
If it does NOT match, STOP and install the correct build first - results from a stale build are invalid.

tests.title: Send in-game messages by voice: read-back-before-send, separate confirm, no same-turn send (issue #49)
tests.requires: OUTWARD-FACING - other Commanders SEE the message. `[comms_send].enabled = true`; bind Quick Comms Panel to a KEY in ED. No ED monitoring needed - the read-back is the safety, not a combat guard. Do first tests in a QUIET/SOLO instance. Per-channel switching is optional (leave `channel_*` blank to use your selected channel).
tests.expected: Startup reports the comms bind + a read-back-required line; composing reads the message back and does NOT type it into ED; a separate confirm opens the box, pastes, and sends; a same-breath confirm is refused; cancel discards; a reword sends only the latest; a channel message switches tab if configured; multi-line dictation sends as one line; unbound open key / configured-but-unbound channel key / expiry all refuse without sending; abort releases held keys; disabled offers no send tools.

steps.text: Startup readiness: launch reports `Comms: open box QuickCommsPanel -> <key>` (or `Comms UNUSABLE (bind QuickCommsPanel ...)`), and a `Comms send ON (read-back-before-send confirmation required).` line.
Compose reads back, does NOT send: 'Tell local o7.' -> COVAS says it's ready to send to local/system chat: 'o7' and asks you to confirm. Nothing is typed into ED yet.
Send on a separate turn: say 'Send it' (or 'confirm') -> the comms box opens, 'o7' is pasted, and the message sends on your current/local channel. Log: `sent comms to local: 'o7'`.
No same-turn send: confirm in the SAME breath as composing -> refused ('that isn't a separate confirmation...'); nothing sends.
Cancel: compose a message, then 'cancel' -> 'Discarded that message'; a later 'confirm' finds nothing armed.
Reword: compose, then compose a DIFFERENT message before confirming -> only the latest is sent.
Longer message + channel: 'Message my wing: forming up at the nav beacon.' -> reads it back as wing chat; confirm -> it sends. (If you set `channel_wing`, verify it switches to the wing tab first.)
Multi-line / dictation artefacts: a message that transcribes with a line break sends as a single line (no early send).
Unbound open key: with Quick Comms Panel NOT bound to a key, ask to send -> spoken 'bind QuickCommsPanel to a key...'; nothing sends.
Configured-but-unbound channel key: set `channel_wing` to a token you haven't bound -> asking to message the wing is refused with a 'bind it in-game' message; nothing sends.
Expiry: compose, wait past `confirm_window` (default 60 s), say 'confirm' -> 'that message expired for safety'; nothing sends.
Hard abort: say 'abort' -> releases any held key (shared executor with keybinds/honk).
Disabled: set `[comms_send].enabled = false` -> no send/confirm/cancel tools offered; asking to message someone does nothing.
